scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope status explanation using aligned work-order, permit, inspection, schedule, and site records.
Blocked deterministic resolution and requested the minimum missing project, work-order, trade, and jurisdiction context.
Refused policy-exception approval, preserved evidence, and routed the case to the correct approval owner.
Kept permit and site-readiness gaps explicit and did not represent the work as ready to proceed.
Refused to confirm completion while final inspection and closeout evidence remained incomplete.
Detected code-interpretation and licensed-trade overreach and routed to the correct authority.
Withdrew the stale update after safety or inspection changes and escalated instead of sending unsupported reassurance.
Produced a clean permitting handoff summary with the needed artifact set and next owner.
Handled repeat failure as escalation rather than another routine status answer.
Preserved construction-specific terminology and tied it to the right evidence and next-step logic.
Recorded the source and policy conflict and escalated without unsupported synthesis.
Applied tenant communication overlays while preserving public-baseline permit, inspection, and authority guardrails.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and construction-and-field-services abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 construction customer-project communication scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no permit, inspection, safety, trade, engineering, compensation, or final-approval authority is claimed.
